text.title: Agents ReAct Pattern — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agents-react-pattern` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Loop step strictly follows Thought → Action → Observation; mixing steps in one LLM call is rejected.
rule.rationale: Skipping the Thought step (going straight to Action) loses interpretability; concatenating Action+Observation in one call breaks the audit log.

rule.statement: Action MUST be a typed tool call (tool_name + Pydantic args). Free-form actions rejected.
rule.rationale: Free-form actions are unparseable; the loop can't dispatch them correctly.

rule.statement: Max iterations capped (default 10). On exceeding, return last observation + uncertainty flag.
rule.rationale: Without cap, models loop indefinitely on hard tasks.

rule.statement: Loop terminates on explicit `FinalAnswer` tool OR on iteration cap. No string heuristic («I think we're done»).
rule.rationale: String heuristics produce false positives and false negatives; explicit termination tool is deterministic.

rule.statement: Every (Thought, Action, Observation) triple is logged with iteration index.
rule.rationale: Without audit, debugging failed runs is impossible at scale.
